rationale: In the literature review and Table 1, Yin et al. (2017) is discussed as the closest prior evidence on arousal and review helpfulness, not merely listed in background citations. The citing paper uses it to motivate a central gap (arousal vs emotionality, and unclear backfiring conditions) and to frame its hypotheses about product-type differences. It also engages the cited finding empirically by explicitly testing for a Yin et al.-style quadratic diminishing-returns pattern and contrasting that with its own results.
relation_description: The citing paper extends Yin et al. (2017) by moving from expressed arousal to expressed emotionality in reviews, adding an expectations-and-trust account, and examining downstream product evaluations and choice. It preserves the core review-helpfulness line of inquiry while introducing different mechanism and boundary-condition claims.
